zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Margaret Bagot於大約1430年出生在英國武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓時，她的父親Knight MP Sir John Bagot74歲，母親Beatrix de Villiers63歲。她於大約1460年與John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1631年住在英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯。她去世於1480年之後在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓，埋葬在英國爾格亞特 布德沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Margaret Bagot于大约1430年出生在英国武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓时，她的父亲Knight MP Sir John Bagot74岁，母亲Beatrix de Villiers63岁。她于大约1460年与John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire在英国英格兰斯塔福爾布羅姆利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1631年住在英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯。她去世于1480年之后在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾貝爾布盧頓，埋葬在英国爾格亞特 布德沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Bagot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Baggett   (see also   Baggott  ), from a pet form of   Bagg   2.

variants.name: Baggett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Norman personal name Bagot, a diminutive of ancient Germanic Bago, from the root bag- ‘to fight’. Compare Bagg 2.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Margaret Bagot was born about 1430, in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England, her father, Knight MP Sir John Bagot, was 74 and her mother, Beatrix de Villiers, was 63. She married John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire about 1460, in Bromley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She lived in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England in 1631. She died after 1480, in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England.

fr.Pendergast, Pembrokeshire, Wales: Prendergast, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Margaret Bagot est née environ 1430, à Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Knight MP Sir John Bagot, avait 74 ans et sa mère, Beatrix de Villiers, avait 63 ans. Elle a épousé John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire environ 1460, à Bromley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle a vécu à Great Budworth, Cheshire, Angleterre en 1631. Elle est décédée après 1480, à Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Great Budworth, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Margaret Bagot um 1430 in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Knight MP Sir John Bagot 74 und ihre Mutter Beatrix de Villiers 63. Sie hat um 1460 in Bromley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1631 hat sie in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England gelebt. Sie starb nach 1480 in Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Great Budworth, Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Margaret Bagot è nata il circa 1430 nel Belbroughton, Worcestershire, England, sua padre, Knight MP Sir John Bagot, aveva 74 anni e sua madre, Beatrix de Villiers, aveva 63 anni. Lei sposato John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire circa 1460, nel Bromley, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Great Budworth, Cheshire, England nel 1631. Morì dopo il 1480, nel Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Great Budworth, Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Margaret Bagotが約1430年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベルブロウトンで生まれた時，父Knight MP Sir John Bagotは74歳，母Beatrix de Villiersは63歳であった。約1460年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアブロムリーでJohn Davenport of Bramhall, Esquireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1631年にはイギリスチェシュアイアグレアト ブドウオルスに住んでいた。彼女は1480年以後にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベルブロウトンで死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアグレアト ブドウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Margaret Bagot(이)가 대략 1430년 영국 워스스터실르 벨브루그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Knight MP Sir John Bagot(은)는 74세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrix de Villiers(은)는 63세였다. 그녀는 대략 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 브롬리에서 John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1631년도에 영국 체실르 그리트 버드워스에서 살았다. 그녀는 1480년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 벨브루그h턴에서 사망했으며 영국 체실르 그리트 버드워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Margaret Bagot nasceu aproximadamente 1430, em Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Knight MP Sir John Bagot, tinha 74 anos e sua mãe, Beatrix de Villiers, tinha 63 anos. Ela casou-se com John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire aproximadamente 1460, em Bromley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela viveu em Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1631. Ela faleceu após 1480, em Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Margaret Bagot родилась около 1430 г. в местности белброугтон, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Knight MP Sir John Bagot было 74 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrix de Villiers, 63. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire около 1460 г. в местности бромли, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она жила в местности грит будворт, чешир, Англия в 1631 году. Она умерла после 1480 г. в местности белброугтон, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности грит будворт, чешир, Англия.

Se casó con John Davenport of Bramhall, Esquire aproximadamente 1460, en Bromley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Vivió en Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra en 1631. Murió después de 1480, en Belbroughton, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Great Budworth, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
